mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal spending and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of early-cycle months, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260, rising inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter future monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or the threshold effect disappears in a piecewise regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly real personal spending confirms broad-based demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >55% of triggered quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below its 2017 baseline of 100, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic momentum, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where INDPRO drops below 100, or the threshold signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward validation.
